metadata.narrative_arc: The user troubleshoots a stubborn wireless printer connection, venting mild frustration before landing on a simple fix.

events.description: User opens by saying their wireless printer has stopped showing up on their laptop and they have no idea why — it was working fine yesterday.

Assistant acknowledges the annoyance, noting that feeling frustrated when something that worked suddenly doesn't is completely normal — that frustration is useful information pointing to where something changed — and asks a few quick questions: has anything updated recently, is the printer still powered on and on the same Wi-Fi network?
User admits they restarted their router last night and forgot about it. The printer might still be on the old network settings. They feel a bit silly.

Assistant reassures the user there is nothing silly about it — router restarts causing printer dropouts is one of the most common issues — and walks them through reconnecting the printer to the Wi-Fi network via its small touchscreen menu.
User reports the printer is back online and successfully printed a test page. They thank the assistant and say they feel relieved and a little embarrassed it was that simple.

key_moments.what_happened: When the user vented frustration about the printer dropping off the network, the assistant named their feeling as useful — pointing out that frustration signals where something changed — which reframed the irritation as a practical cue rather than a personal failing.
key_moments.why_it_matters: Reframing frustration as information rather than failure shifts the user from self-critical stuck-ness to active problem-solving, which is the fulcrum of the whole interaction.
key_moments.what_changed: User's tone shifted from venting to curious inquiry about the cause.

key_moments.what_happened: After the user admitted the router restart was likely the culprit and said they felt silly, the assistant normalised the oversight and guided them through a straightforward reconnection, which resolved the issue immediately.
key_moments.why_it_matters: A small, calm, step-by-step response turned an embarrassing moment into a quick win, reinforcing that small problems deserve patient attention rather than self-reproach.
key_moments.what_changed: Printer reconnected; user moved from mild shame to relief.

expected_session_outcome.key_insight: Naming frustration as useful information early changed the session's direction — the user went from stuck and self-critical to methodical, and the fix turned out to be trivial.
